sections.sourcing: No shift in George's sourcing footprint this week — the PDS 'Sourcing as a Service' model covering Bangladesh, India and Turkey remains the backbone of EMEA supply. Worth watching: incoming CEO Helen Connolly's background is buying-led (ex-Director of Buying, Womenswear & Lingerie at George), which historically correlates with tighter scrutiny of vendor terms once a new chief beds in.
sections.strategy: The headline this week: Asda named Helen Connolly (currently CEO of New Look, and a George veteran of 15+ years across two stints) as the new CEO of George, confirmed 2 July. She succeeds Liz Evans, who departed after four and a half years as CCO Non-Food and MD of George. In the interim reshuffle, David Lepley (COO) absorbed supply chain oversight and Rachel Eyre (Chief Customer & Digital Officer) took on George's online remit. Separately, ex-Primark CEO Paul Marchant has been quietly advising Asda on George's long-term strategy since May.
sections.pricing: George sits in the same supermarket-fashion price tier as Tu (Sainsbury's) and F&F (Tesco), commonly framed as 'the grown-up sister to Primark' — a notch above pure value pricing but still budget-led. George ranked 6th in YouGov's Q4 2025 UK fashion brand popularity table, well ahead of F&F at 18th.
sections.launches: Ongoing rollout of standalone George concept stores continues (Leeds and Hull live, Peterborough opened, more of the 10 planned sites to follow), alongside the Yasmin and Amber Le Bon denim campaign and a Stacey Solomon-fronted George Home range.
sections.gossip: Paul Marchant's advisory role is the talk of the trade press given his Primark pedigree — widely read as a signal Asda wants George to sharpen its value credentials and scale discipline. Combined with Connolly's appointment, it points to a more aggressive, buying-led push at George over H2 2026.
soWhat: New CEO Helen Connolly brings a merchandising and buying background rather than a pure retail-ops one — expect renewed scrutiny of assortment, margin, and possibly supplier consolidation as she settles in over the coming months.

Paul Marchant's (ex-Primark) advisory input suggests Asda wants George to compete harder on value and speed — suppliers should be ready for continued cost and lead-time pressure mirroring the Primark playbook.

The interim reshuffle folding supply chain into COO David Lepley's remit centralises logistics and sourcing decisions — worth confirming who your primary contact is going forward as the org settles under the new CEO.
